--- title: "Design Matrix for interaction effects" date: "2025-02-06" description: "Matrix Algebra" categories: [code, analysis] --- ## Design Matrix for interaction effects Problem discussed with Niklaus Meier (Thank you.) Assume we want interaction terms of continuous variables (variables in $\mathbf{X}$) with categorical variables (variables in $\mathbf{J}$). The corresponding Design Matrix is the **Transposed Khatri-Rao Product**; this is a **row-by-row Kronecker product** of two matrices. $$ \mathbf{J} \bullet \mathbf{X} = \begin{bmatrix} \mathbf{J}[1,] \otimes \mathbf{X}[1,]\\\hline \mathbf{J}[2,] \otimes \mathbf{X}[2,]\\\hline \mathbf{J}[3,] \otimes \mathbf{X}[3,]\\\hline ...\\\hline \mathbf{J}[n,] \otimes \mathbf{X}[n,]\\ \end{bmatrix} $$ # Dummy Matrix $J$ and Matrix $X$, Parameter vector ::: {.cell .column-page} ```{.r .cell-code} library(Matrix) library(psych) n<-20 p1<-2 p2<-4 ``` ::: ::: {.cell .column-page} ```{.r .cell-code} k<-p1*p2 J<-matrix(sample(c(0,1),n*p1,replace=TRUE),n,p1) X<-matrix(round(rnorm(n*p2,10,2)),n,p2) beta<-t(t(rnorm(k))) J ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [,1] [,2] [1,] 1 1 [2,] 1 0 [3,] 1 1 [4,] 1 0 [5,] 0 1 [6,] 0 0 [7,] 1 0 [8,] 1 0 [9,] 0 0 [10,] 1 1 [11,] 1 1 [12,] 1 0 [13,] 0 0 [14,] 0 1 [15,] 1 0 [16,] 0 1 [17,] 1 1 [18,] 0 0 [19,] 1 1 [20,] 0 1 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} X ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [1,] 9 8 12 10 [2,] 9 10 11 11 [3,] 8 9 9 9 [4,] 10 11 10 11 [5,] 10 13 11 9 [6,] 11 6 10 13 [7,] 5 15 8 7 [8,] 7 9 5 9 [9,] 8 10 9 12 [10,] 10 12 13 11 [11,] 8 12 11 11 [12,] 13 8 8 9 [13,] 7 12 10 6 [14,] 12 12 11 8 [15,] 4 10 6 11 [16,] 12 11 8 10 [17,] 12 10 10 10 [18,] 9 9 10 9 [19,] 10 9 7 15 [20,] 11 11 10 7 ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell .column-page} ```{.r .cell-code} beta ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [,1] [1,] 1.21479 [2,] -0.56697 [3,] 0.17182 [4,] -0.04816 [5,] 0.70657 [6,] -0.02747 [7,] 1.32019 [8,] -0.87408 ``` ::: ::: # Design Matrix: Transposed Khatri-Rao product This is a **row-by-row Kronecker product of two matrices** ::: {.cell .column-page} ```{.r .cell-code} Design<-t(KhatriRao(t(J),t(X)))##Design matrix ``` ::: # Alternative ("by hand") ::: {.cell .column-page} ```{.r .cell-code} KR<-matrix(0,rep(n*p1*p2),nrow=n,ncol=p1*p2) for (i in 1:n) {KR[i,]<-kronecker(J[i,],X[i,])} ##Explicit: row-by-row Kronecker product of two matrices headTail(KR) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display}
| |X1 |X2 |X3 |X4 |X5 |X6 |X7 |X8 | |:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---| |1 |9 |8 |12 |10 |9 |8 |12 |10 | |2 |9 |10 |11 |11 |0 |0 |0 |0 | |3 |8 |9 |9 |9 |8 |9 |9 |9 | |4 |10 |11 |10 |11 |0 |0 |0 |0 | |... |... |... |... |... |... |... |... |... | |17 |12 |10 |10 |10 |12 |10 |10 |10 | |18 |0 |0 |0 |0 |0 |0 |0 |0 | |19 |10 |9 |7 |15 |10 |9 |7 |15 | |20 |0 |0 |0 |0 |11 |11 |10 |7 |
::: ::: # Model Matrix (macht dasselbe) ::: {.cell .column-page} ```{.r .cell-code} headTail(model.matrix(~X:J-1)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display}
| |X1.J1 |X2.J1 |X3.J1 |X4.J1 |X1.J2 |X2.J2 |X3.J2 |X4.J2 | |:---|:-----|:-----|:-----|:-----|:-----|:-----|:-----|:-----| |1 |9 |8 |12 |10 |9 |8 |12 |10 | |2 |9 |10 |11 |11 |0 |0 |0 |0 | |3 |8 |9 |9 |9 |8 |9 |9 |9 | |4 |10 |11 |10 |11 |0 |0 |0 |0 | |... |... |... |... |... |... |... |... |... | |17 |12 |10 |10 |10 |12 |10 |10 |10 | |18 |0 |0 |0 |0 |0 |0 |0 |0 | |19 |10 |9 |7 |15 |10 |9 |7 |15 | |20 |0 |0 |0 |0 |11 |11 |10 |7 |
::: ::: # Linear Prediction ::: {.cell .column-page} ```{.r .cell-code} eta<-Design%*%beta Y<-rnorm(n,mean=as.vector(eta),sd=.001) ``` ::: # Analyse ::: {.cell .column-page} ```{.r .cell-code} dat<-data.frame(Y=Y,X=as.data.frame(KR)) data.frame(estimated=coef(m<-lm(Y~.-1,dat)),true=beta) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display}
| | estimated| true| |:----|---------:|-------:| |X.V1 | 1.2148| 1.2148| |X.V2 | -0.5670| -0.5670| |X.V3 | 0.1718| 0.1718| |X.V4 | -0.0481| -0.0482| |X.V5 | 0.7067| 0.7066| |X.V6 | -0.0276| -0.0275| |X.V7 | 1.3204| 1.3202| |X.V8 | -0.8742| -0.8741|
::: :::
